text:
Once upon a time in the tranquil village of Eldergrove, there lived a curious and adventurous mouse named Mortimer. Mortimer was not your typical rodent. Instead of scampering through walls and under tables, he crafted intricate tapestries depicting woodland adventures. His favorite creation was a tapestry depicting one of his own escapades—a daring rafting trip down the shimmering Brookberry River.

One sun-drenched afternoon, Mortimer decided to embark on a new adventure. Determined to create an even more magnificent tapestry, he collected yarn threads of every color he could find from the village fair—one golden, one crimson, one teal, one purple, one cobalt blue. Then, he snuck out of his home, his tiny feet barely making a sound on the leaf-strewn path.

After hours of unwinding his ropes across sturdy branches and balancing delicate leaves onto his makeshift scaffold, Mortimer felt ready to unveil his masterpiece. He sat back on his platform, which had
